author: Louie, Ryan and Shah, Raj Sanjay and Orney, Ifdita Hasan and Pacheco, Juan Pablo and Brunskill, Emma and Yang, Diyi
title: Can LLM-Simulated Practice and Feedback Upskill Human Counselors? A Randomized Study with 90+ Novice Counselors
abstract: The growing demand for accessible mental health support requires training more counselors, yet existing approaches remain resource-intensive and difficult to scale. LLMs can realistically simulate patients and generate actionable feedback for training, but their actual impact on novice counselor skill development remains unknown...

title: Five Problems in Synthetic Users Created Using LLMs
user_notes: 1. Compression problem: synthetic user simplifies human experience too much. 2. Extrapolation problem: lacks underlying data, hallucinated responses increase. 3. Circularity problem: deductive approach just mirrors what you programmed. 4. Unpredictability problem: human experience is unpredictable in ways different from computational randomness. 5. Double work problem: if you have validation data, why not use it directly?

author: Lujain
title: AI Mode Study: Always Available, Not Judgmental, Private, Validating
user_notes: AI mode is: always available, not judgmental, seemingly private, encouraging (validating). Responsiveness, certainty (relationship). Feeling known was a mediator. Cost-benefit framework for AI vs human interaction. Study findings: people want highly personalized AI, understood me best and easiest to talk to. Takeaway: no substitution per se, but a new standard and changes perceptions of offline relationships.
